key_items: Bitcoin most oversold since 2020 crash with RSI mirroring setups that preceded 50% rebounds to $70K
Bitcoin has dumped all gains since Trump reelection with 50% decline from peak, testing institutional adoption narrative
Multiple technical indicators suggest $60K mining cost floor and potential capitulation bottom, but $35B realized losses still below 2022 bear market total indicating deeper losses possible
reasoning: Bitcoin shows extreme oversold conditions and technical capitulation signals that historically precede bounces, yet macroeconomic headwinds (Treasury liquidity drain, Fed rate-cut reversal, Nasdaq correction risks) and incomplete capitulation metrics (realized losses below 2022 levels) create conflicting signals for the 1-7 day swing timeframe. The setup favors tactical bounces toward $64.5K-$70K rather than sustained rallies, with elevated downside risk if macroeconomic data disappoints.
risks: A Treasury cash rebuild exceeding $900B combined with deeper Nasdaq correction could trigger fresh liquidations below the $60K support level, negating capitulation-buy signals and risking a test toward the $50K-$55K electrical cost floor.
